description: Local-first MCP server that serves coding agents one canonical doc per query — a path:line pointer with a canonical/stale/duplicate freshness marker — instead of rereading the repo. Indexes your markdown, routes a question to the single current doc, and gives agents a shared write-back store.
rules: Install with `uv tool install trovex`, then `trovex index <repo>` to index a repo's markdown.
trovex is an MCP server: run `trovex serve` and connect over HTTP at http://localhost:8765/mcp.
Call the `trovex(query)` tool to get the one canonical doc that answers a question (a path:line pointer with a canonical/stale/duplicate freshness marker), not a pile of files to rank.
Use `trovex_write` / `trovex_read` for the shared agent-memory store; `trovex_search` for passage-level retrieval with tag filters.
Local-first: indexing, embeddings (ONNX) and vector search (sqlite-vec) run on your machine with no cloud or API key.
Verify token savings on your own repo with `trovex bench <repo>` (add `--eval` for the answer+judge A/B). Method and numbers: https://trovex.dev/measure
